🚪 Exit Alerts & Position Detail
🔔 ANET — Arista Networks — 2.2% FROM TP1 $185 ACTION NOW
ATH (Jul 8 close):~$181.05 Entry avg:$157.79
TP1 target:$185.00 (full exit) Distance:$3.95 = 2.2%
Stop:$135.00 Hard exit deadline:Friday July 24
💵 Commission math at TP1 ($185)
Sell 1.27 shares @ $185.00
Gross P&L: ($185.00 − $157.79) × 1.27 = +$34.56
Exit commission: − $3.00
Net P&L: +$31.56 ✓
At current ~$181: gross +$29.54 | net +$26.54 if exiting today
Why $185 is a real level: Analysts at KeyBanc, BofA, Morgan Stanley all raised targets to $190–$200 on Jul 6–7, with consensus at ~$189. The $185 zone = pre-earnings analyst cluster & resistance from the prior May swing high. It's where institutions will distribute some size. The bull case above $185 needs Q2 beat (Aug 5).

Your options:
① Set limit sell at $185 now. Fills automatically if hit. Clean, no discipline required.
② Raise TP to $195 (split the $185–$200 analyst range). Exit by July 24 regardless. Risk: earnings overhang grows from Aug 5.
③ Hold to July 24 exit regardless of price. With ANET at ATH, you're exiting at roughly $181 (est.). Net: +$26.54. Still clean.

Recommended: Option 1. TP1 at $185 was set deliberately. The stock is 2.2% away. Set the limit. If it runs past $185 and you miss some upside, that's a good problem. Your call.
Halal status: ⚠️ Unverified — check Musaffa before re-entry post-exit. Likely compliant.
Q2 earnings: ~Aug 5, 2026. Exit deadline July 24 (last trading day before July 25 weekend) keeps you out of earnings risk.
News context (Jul 6–8): 1.6Tbps AI fabric switch launch, Meta/Microsoft/Oracle/AMD partnerships. AI Ethernet TAM expansion. Demand "exceptional" per management.
✅ VERA — Vera Therapeutics — Day 2 Post-Approval. HOLD.
FDA decision:APPROVED July 7 (Trutakna™ — accelerated) Day 2 price est:~$42.52 (Jul 8 AH)
Entry:$41.78 avg × 8 shares P&L est:+$5.96 gross / +$2.96 net
Stop (updated):$38.00 — update in IBKR ⚠ TP1 / TP2:$55 (4 shares) / $78 (4 shares)
Stop: $38.00 — MUST UPDATE in IBKR. The old stop ($24.50) was a CRL gap-down target — irrelevant post-approval. $38 = pre-PDUFA floor (Jun 29–Jul 6 consolidation zone). Running broker stop at $24.50 gives you false comfort on a 12% slide before any alert.
At TP1 ($55): Gross ($55−$41.78)×4 = +$52.88 | Commission −$3.00 | Net +$49.88 ✓
At TP2 ($78): Gross ($78−$41.78)×4 = +$144.88 | Commission −$3.00 | Net +$141.88 ✓
⚠️ New binary event on VERA: Accelerated approval requires ORIGIN 3 confirmatory eGFR data (expected Q3 2026). If ORIGIN 3 misses kidney function endpoint → FDA can withdraw approval. This is a new catalyst date to track. When announced, treat it like the original PDUFA: pre-event sizing decision, stop, probability assessment. Run the same playbook you ran on July 7.
After TP1: move stop on remaining 4 shares to $42 (entry cost lock-in). Free ride to TP2 from there. Run portfolio-manager to update stop to $38.
⏳ Watchlist Status — All Items Archived

AdCom: July 29, 2026 (20 days) · PDUFA: Aug 22, 2026 · Drug: Deramiocel (DMD cardiomyopathy)

Do not enter regardless of setup quality. Remove from active watchlist via portfolio-manager.

📁 VERA (watchlist) — Archived. Active position above. Remove from watchlist.
📡 Catalyst Plays — Thursday July 9, 2026
15 parallel searches run. Gate 1: event ≤45 days. Gate 2: market cap <$2B. Gate 3: halal pre-screen.
Dropped at Gate 2 (>$2B market cap):
CORT (Relacorilant, PDUFA July 11 / Mon Jul 13 effective) — market cap ~$9.4B → FAIL
CELC (Gedatolisib, PDUFA July 17) — market cap ~$5.3B → FAIL
Dropped at Gate 3 (halal): CAPR (9.9% interest income) → FAIL
OTLK — Outlook Therapeutics 🌑 WATCH · 3/5
Catalyst: BLA resubmission — ONS-5010 / Lytenava™ (bevacizumab-vikg) for wet AMD (nAMD)
PDUFA date: July 29, 2026 — 20 days away · 🌒 WATCH
Review class: Class 1 (2-month goal) — FDA confirmed it accepted limited scope resubmission addressing specific CRL deficiencies
International: Already approved in Germany & UK (Lytenava) — positive signal for US resubmission
Market cap: Micro-cap (unconfirmed from IBKR — estimated <$50M based on category) · Float: unverified
Probability: ~68% — Class 1 resubmission base rate when FDA previously accepted efficacy. Risk: FDA finds CMC or manufacturing deficiency not fully addressed.
Scenarios:
✓ Approval: First ophthalmic bevacizumab approved in US → micro-cap +100–300%+ typical
✗ CRL again: Another complete response → -50 to -70% likely
Smart money: No institutional signal found. Pure binary on resubmission outcome.
Halal: ⚠️ Unverified — pharmaceutical, no obvious disqualifiers. Check Musaffa before entry.
Dilution risk: Micro-cap with no product revenue yet — shelf registration risk. Check 10-Q cash runway.
💡 If entering: Verify halal on Musaffa first. 30% cap on portfolio (~$250 max). PDUFA July 29 (Wednesday). Set stop at 30% below entry as hard floor. Say “analyze OTLK” for full deep dive.
MNKD — MannKind Corporation 🌑 WATCH · 2/5
Catalyst: sNDA for FUROSCIX ReadyFlow™ Autoinjector
PDUFA date: July 26, 2026 (Sun → effective Monday July 27) — 18 days · 🌑 SOON
What it is: New delivery device for already-approved FUROSCIX (SC furosemide 80mg/mL) — delivers in <10 seconds vs hours for existing on-body infusor
Market cap: ~$1.21B · Pass Gate 2
Binary risk assessment: LOW binary. This is a device sNDA for an existing approved drug — not a new molecular entity. FDA probability ~88–92%. Upside on approval: +10–25% (device approval pop for existing drug). Downside on CRL: -10–20%. Limited squeeze potential.
Q1 2026 revenue: Up 15% YoY as Furoscix ramps. CHF/CKD market is large but competitive.
Halal: ⚠️ Unverified. Check Musaffa before entry.
Verdict: Lower conviction than OTLK because the binary is weak. But lower downside too. If Abbas wants a less risky FDA play, MNKD has better probability. If he wants meaningful upside, OTLK is the pick. Don't own both — limited cash.
MSFT — Microsoft · Tier A Large-Cap Fallback (post-ANET rotation) 📊 TIER A FALLBACK
No small-cap catalyst plays met all 3 gates — Tier A fallback for post-ANET rotation.
Logic: When ANET exits (~July 24), freed capital (~$230 net) + existing cash = ~$490 deployable. MSFT is the highest-conviction Tier A candidate: Azure AI demand, ~$75B annual FCF, AAOIFI-likely compliant, Q4 FY2026 earnings ~late July (confirm date before sizing). Anchor position $200–250 to diversify from biotech concentration.
Halal: Generally AAOIFI-compliant. Verify on Musaffa before entry.
Say “analyze MSFT” to get full 8-section deep dive. Confirm Q4 earnings date before committing size.
💵 Cash Deployment — $265.41 available
Available cash$265.41
Minimum reserve (1 exit commission)−$3.00
Buy commission (if deploying)−$3.00
Net deployable$259.41
30% concentration cap (est. portfolio ~$835)≤$250 new position
💡 Recommended: Hold cash — deploy post-ANET exit
Option A — OTLK now ($250 max): Halal check first. 20 days to PDUFA (July 29). Moderate conviction (3/5). Micro-cap, real binary. Risk: prior CRL, dilution, limited cash runway. Your call.
Option B — Hold (recommended): ANET exits ~July 24. Freed cash ($229 net) + $265 = ~$494 deployable. At $494, you can enter MSFT ($250) and still have $244 reserve. Better sizing, better diversification. No urgency to deploy pre-ANET exit.

Math: Using all $260 on OTLK now leaves you with $5 reserve and VERA risk still running. If VERA drops to stop ($38) you need to sell fast. Hold cash → you have $265 buffer if VERA runs into trouble. Patience wins here.

🔮 Position Risk Scores (Step 3.6)

ANET 38/100 MODERATE Premium valuation (~40x fwd EPS) · Strong FCF/ROIC but approaching exit · Q2 earnings overhang Aug 5
HIGH on VERA does not trigger rotation — ORIGIN 3 is not imminent. Hold. If ORIGIN 3 date is announced, re-run the pre-PDUFA risk playbook.
📚 Equity Education · Concept #8 of 30 US equity mechanics — anchored to your positions
Return on Invested Capital (ROIC)
ROIC = NOPAT ÷ Invested Capital     (NOPAT = net operating profit after tax · Invested Capital = debt + equity)
ROIC is the single best quality signal in equity analysis. It answers: for every dollar the business has absorbed, how much profit does it generate? The reason it matters is structural — if a business earns 40% ROIC while competitors earn 10%, capital should flood in and equalize returns. The fact it doesn’t means something is blocking competition: a patent moat, network effects, switching costs, pricing power. That permanent barrier is the moat you’re paying for when you buy a premium-multiple stock.

ANET (your current holding, exits July 24): ROIC is approximately 45–50%. No debt, ~40% net margins, asset-light model (fabless — TSMC manufactures, Arista designs). Every dollar put into the business generates $0.45–$0.50 in after-tax profit. That’s why KeyBanc can model a $200 price target without stretching credibility — the ROIC justifies the premium. When ANET exits and you redeploy into MSFT, compare ROIC: MSFT runs ~30–35% ROIC (cloud margins + Azure leverage). Lower than ANET, still excellent.

VERA (your other holding): ROIC is not measurable yet — pre-revenue biotechs by definition have negative returns on capital (cash burn > returns). Post-Trutakna approval, watch for the inflection point: the first quarter where gross margin on atacicept sales starts covering SG&A. That’s when VERA transitions from “binary option on FDA” to “ROIC story.” Usually 6–18 months post-launch.

Rule of thumb: Consistent ROIC >20% over 3+ years = likely moat. 10–20% = competitive business, no structural advantage. <10% = commodity. The best time to buy a high-ROIC business is when its stock temporarily underperforms due to a solvable problem (supply constraints, macro pressure) while ROIC stays high. ANET’s supply note in Q2 guidance is exactly that kind of temporary noise — ROIC is intact.
Also today: WACC (Weighted Average Cost of Capital) — ROIC only matters relative to WACC. ROIC > WACC = value being created for shareholders. ROIC < WACC = value being destroyed even if the company is “profitable.” ANET’s WACC is ~7–9%, ROIC ~47% — gap of ~38 points. That gap is the moat in quantitative form.
